description_long: This is a medium-sized bird with long, broad wings and a streamlined body, resembling a gull. Its plumage is primarily white with gray tones on the wings and back, and it has black-tipped primary feathers. The head is white, and the beak is yellow and slightly hooked at the tip, typical of many urban-dwelling gulls. The legs are dark, almost black, and the tail is short and slightly rounded. The bird's wings are outstretched, showing detailed feather structure and subtle color gradations. This bird is commonly seen in urban environments, especially near water, parks, and rooftops.
description: A medium-sized gull-like bird with white and gray plumage, yellow beak, and black-tipped wings, often seen in urban areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bird's outstretched wings, white head, and yellow beak.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bird's streamlined body, yellow beak, and dark legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full span of the wings, black-tipped feathers, and rounded tail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bird's profile, wing structure, and leg position.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid white head and underparts, gray wings with black-tipped primaries, and subtle mottling on the back
